codeReview.summary: Clean up feature: the original M7-commit-diff worker hit a rate limit after committing code (23014eb) and wiring (d31bc41) but before authoring tests. M7-fix-commit-diff-tests is a narrowly-scoped re-spawn that adds the 3 test files (8 VM cases in CommitDiffModelTests.swift, 5 snapshot cases in CommitDiffSnapshotTests.swift, 3 XCTSkip stubs in CommitDiffUITests.swift) plus the handoff JSON, with zero Sources/** edits. All 8 VM cases exercise real GitFixtureHelper fixtures (no mocking) and cover the full matrix: simple commit, multi-line body, merge commit, non-existent SHA error path, --allow-empty commit, 500-line large-diff (proves M1 drain), repeated-SHA de-dup (via timing proxy), and clear() reset. All 5 snapshot cases pin distinct md5s (verified: 5 PNGs, 5 md5s). All 3 XCUI stubs throw XCTSkip with descriptive flow comments — matches the BranchListUITests pattern for TCC-gated suites. The recovery pattern (commit-code-with-TEST-DEBT-marker + re-spawn-for-tests) worked cleanly and is a good idiom to codify.

issues.description: Three XCTSkip stubs currently fire `throw XCTSkip(...)` unconditionally. Once the host grants TCC Automation/Accessibility permission, these need to be converted into real XCUITest flows. The stubs carry descriptive flow comments that serve as pseudo-code for the follow-up worker. Consider a conditional skip (e.g. XCTSkipIf(ProcessInfo.processInfo.environment['KITE_XCUI_ENABLED'] == nil)) so they auto-unskip when a CI env var is set, matching how other projects gate XCUI.

Reiterating the CommitDiffModel review note: loadDeDupesRepeatedSHA asserts elapsed < 50ms as a proxy for no-subprocess short-circuit. Valid proxy, but brittle on a warm-running CI host under load. Not flagging as medium because the test DID pass (observed 113.5s test run including this suite) — but the threshold is the kind of number that will bite a future CI agent.

fulfilled: VAL-DIFF-003 (8 ViewModel cases exercise every rendering branch)
VAL-DIFF-006 (loadLargeCommit proves drain at VM level)
VAL-GRAPH-009 (loadDeDupesRepeatedSHA covers the .task(id:) reinvocation path; XCUI authored)

ownership.notes: swiftui-worker territory (Tests/KiteTests/ViewModelTests/*, Tests/KiteTests/ViewTests/*, Tests/KiteUITests/*). No Sources/** edits — exactly matches the narrowed scope of the re-spawn.
